summary: Closed, unmerged first-pass PR for matching and organizing the melee `lb` library. The slice shows a large new C translation unit for the former `code_8000B074` area under `src/melee/lb/lbunknown_001.c`, build-list updates, `lb` header moves from `src/melee/lb` to `include/melee/lb`, include path normalization to `<melee/lb/...>`, assembly file renames from address-based `code_...` names to `lbunknown_00N`, and decomp.me scratch links added to `lbanim.s`. No PR body or review comments were present, so reviewer intent and merge-blocking reasons are not evidenced.
what_changed: Added a 1019-line `src/melee/lb/lbunknown_001.c` implementing many functions in the `0x8000B074` region, including JObj/AObj animation helpers, DObj animation rate/request helpers, frame accessors, constraint RObj helpers, and related static tables.

Renamed address-based assembly files to library-scoped unknown names: `code_8000CE50.s` to `lbunknown_002.s`, `code_8000F9F8.s` to `lbunknown_003.s`, `code_80019230.s` to `lbunknown_004.s`, and `code_80019880.s` to `lbunknown_005.s`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to use `lbunknown_001.c.o` and the renamed `lbunknown_00N.s.o` files, and added `src/melee/lb/lbanim.c.o` before `asm/melee/lb/lbanim.s.o`.

Moved several `lb` headers from `src/melee/lb` into `include/melee/lb`, including `lbarchive.h`, `lbcommand.h`, `lbfile.h`, `lblanguage.h`, `lbrefract.h`, `lbtime.h`, `lbvector.h`, and the former `code_8000B074.h` as `lbunknown_001.h`.
Removed direct `#include <global.h>` from several moved headers, leaving more specific Dolphin/type includes where shown.

Changed local quoted includes in `lbcommand.c`, `lblanguage.c`, `lbtime.c`, and the former `code_8000B074.c` area to angle-bracket project includes such as `<melee/lb/lbcommand.h>` and `<melee/lb/lbunknown_001.h>`.

Added a skeletal `src/melee/lb/lbanim.c` and `include/melee/lb/lbanim.h`; the C file only declares externs for `local_8001E560`, `local_8001E60C`, and `func_8001E7E8` while assembly remains in `lbanim.s`.
Added decomp.me scratch URLs as comments above four `lbanim.s` routines: `local_8001E560`, `local_8001E60C`, `func_8001E6D8`, and `func_8001E7E8`.
Adjusted an address comment for `GXInit` in `src/dolphin/gx/GXInit.c` from `0033733C` to `00337360`.
smart_moves: Grouped unknown `lb` files under semantic library-prefixed names (`lbunknown_001`, `lbunknown_002`, etc.) instead of raw `code_ADDRESS` filenames, which makes the object list and future search/navigation more coherent even before final symbol names are known.

Moved reusable `lb` headers into `include/melee/lb` and converted users to `<melee/lb/...>` includes, reducing dependence on source-directory-local headers and aligning with broader project include layout.

Kept provisional comments for likely semantic names such as `lbDObjSetRateAll`, `lbDObjReqAnimAll`, `lbGetJObjFramerate`, `lbGetJObjCurrFrame`, and `lbGetJObjEndFrame` without renaming exported `func_...` symbols in the diff, preserving match-oriented caution.
Recorded decomp.me scratch links directly in `lbanim.s`, making active matching attempts traceable for later contributors.
Used small inline wrappers such as `jobj_parent` and `robj_next` in the new C source, suggesting a tactic for shaping generated code around null-check/control-flow patterns.
decomp_lessons: For partially understood library regions, prefer library-scoped provisional filenames over address-only names, but keep function symbols conservative until names are evidenced.
Header moves should be paired with include-path updates and build-list updates in the same PR to avoid stale local includes and object path drift.

When lifting large animation/helper regions, preserve baselib type usage (`HSD_JObj`, `HSD_AObj`, `HSD_DObj`, `HSD_RObj`, etc.) rather than flattening to `void*`; this improves readability and exposes real structure dependencies.
Removing broad `global.h` includes from public headers is useful when the header can rely on narrower type headers, but future work should verify that every moved header remains self-contained.
Leaving scratch links in assembly can be valuable evidence for in-progress matches, especially when only part of a module has been moved into C.
The slice has no review comments and the PR was closed without merge, so its tactics should be treated as historical evidence rather than accepted project policy.
naming_conventions: Address-only files such as `code_8000B074` and `code_8000CE50` were converted toward `lbunknown_00N` names for unknown chunks within the `lb` library.
Project headers were included with angle-bracket paths under `melee/lb`, for example `<melee/lb/lbcommand.h>` and `<melee/lb/lbunknown_001.h>`.

Header guards in existing moved headers retained lowercase underscore styles such as `_lbarchive_h_`, `_lbcommands_h_`, `_lbfile_h_`, `_lblanguage_h_`, and `_lbtime_h_`; the newly added `lbanim.h` used `melee_lb_lbanim_h`.
Provisional semantic names were left as comments near still-numbered functions, for example `// lbDObjSetRateAll` above `func_8000BA44` and `// lbGetJObjCurrFrame` above `func_8000BDB4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added decomp.me scratch references for individual `lbanim.s` routines rather than attempting to replace them all in this slice.
Introduced a C object for `lbanim.c` while leaving corresponding assembly present, indicating a staged setup for future lbanim matching.
Used static data arrays with original `lbl_803B...` labels in `lbunknown_001.c`, preserving data identity while matching code around them.
Used inline helper functions to influence compiler output for null-safe field access patterns.
Preserved many exported function names as `func_8000....` while adding comments for suspected real names, a low-risk approach when semantic naming evidence is incomplete.
The diff shows at least one `asm void func_8000C490(...)` block in the new C file, implying mixed C/inline-assembly was used for a still-unmatched or hard-to-match routine.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged or replaced `src/melee/lb/lbunknown_001.c` work from PR 445.
Search for final names of `func_8000BA44`, `func_8000BB24`, `func_8000BD28`, `func_8000BDB4`, and `func_8000BE40`.
Check whether the `lbunknown_00N` file naming convention persisted or was superseded by more specific `lb` module names.
Look for reviews or follow-up commits explaining why PR 445 was closed without merge.
Audit later header organization around `include/melee/lb` for self-contained include expectations and `global.h` removal patterns.
